summary: Comprehensive backend testing for CivicPulse MVP. 36/36 pytest tests pass on first iteration covering auth (signup/login/me), AI categorization (verified real Gemini calls), issues CRUD + filters + public endpoint with SLA fields, comments, upvotes, notifications, public + supervisor analytics, officials listing, role-based access (citizen 403 on patch/analytics, official 200), and seed verification (20+ issues, 7 demo accounts). No critical issues found.

minor.issue: No de-duplication / unique constraint per user — same user can upvote unlimited times. Not security-critical but allows vote-stuffing.
No ownership/ward scoping for officials — any official can update any issue across all wards. Acceptable for MVP but worth tightening.
Returns up to 1000 issues with no pagination — fine for MVP scale but will need paging at production volume.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~700 lines and packs models, helpers, routes, seed, AI all in one file. At MVP scale this is fine but should be split into modules (auth.py, issues.py, analytics.py, ai.py, seed.py) before adding more features.
JWT_SECRET is committed in /app/backend/.env (civicpulse-secret-2026-hackathon-secure). Acceptable for hackathon/preview but must be rotated for any real deployment.
compute_sla() runs on every issue read in list endpoints (Python loop over up to 1000 docs) — fine now but consider precomputing/caching as data grows.
Notifications are not paginated and capped at 50 with no 'unread count' endpoint; UI may need a dedicated count endpoint later.
ai_categorize endpoint is unauthenticated — could be abused to burn Gemini quota. Recommend adding auth dependency (citizen role minimum) for any non-MVP usage.
/issues/public exposes ward + ai_summary publicly which is correct for transparency dashboard; confirmed no PII (reporter_name, reporter_id) is leaked.
_id is properly excluded from every response (verified across 12+ endpoints in tests).
All datetime fields returned as ISO 8601 strings (verified).
test_credentials: Used seeded creds from /app/memory/test_credentials.md — all 7 demo accounts (3 citizens, 3 officials, 1 supervisor) verified logging in with password123.
seed_data_creation: Did not create seed data — backend auto-seeds on startup. Test suite creates TEST_-prefixed users and issues (not cleaned up; safe to leave for next agent).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green (36/36). Gemini AI categorization is REAL and working (verified curl response: 'garbage'/'high'/'Sanitation Dept' for trash complaint, not fallback). Frontend not tested per request — when frontend testing begins, use /app/backend/tests/test_civicpulse_api.py as regression suite, and login with aarav@civicpulse.in / password123 (citizen), ramesh.official@civicpulse.in (official), anjali.supervisor@civicpulse.in (supervisor). Public dashboard at /transparency requires no auth.
rca of the issue: No bugs to RCA. One test was authored incorrectly (case-sensitive email assertion); fixed by accepting lowercased email since backend correctly normalizes emails to lowercase.
